scope_note: QA Pythagorean Gnomon and Square Identities cert [338]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1993) Pythagorean Arithmetic Vol I, pp.37-39 and 43-46. Identity claims: F=d^2-e^2; C=2be+2e^2; C^2=4E^2+4EF; A,B are 5-par; D,E have opposite par-types. (C1) F=d^2-e^2=(d+e)(d-e)=ab since b=d-e, a=d+e; 11 pairs. (C2) C=2de=2be+2e^2=2e(b+e) three-form equivalence. (C3) C^2=4*E^2+4*E*F where E=e^2; 10 pairs. (C4) A=a^2 and B=b^2 are 5-par (≡1 mod 4) since a,b odd; 11 pairs. (C5) D=d^2 and E=e^2 always have opposite par-types (4-par vs 5-par) since d,e have opposite parities; 12 pairs. DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
